content: Treatment determined by resectability. Resectable (no arterial contact, patent SMV/PV): upfront surgery (Whipple procedure for head, distal pancreatectomy for body/tail) followed by adjuvant chemotherapy. Modified FOLFIRINOX preferred for fit patients (PRODIGE 24 trial, median OS 54.4 months); gemcitabine + capecitabine alternative. Borderline resectable (limited arterial contact, reconstructible vein involvement): neoadjuvant chemotherapy (FOLFIRINOX or gemcitabine + nab-paclitaxel) ± radiation, then restaging for surgery. Locally advanced (unresectable, no metastases): systemic chemotherapy primary; consider consolidation chemoradiation for local control; some convert to resectable. Metastatic: first-line FOLFIRINOX (fit patients) or gemcitabine + nab-paclitaxel; second-line nanoliposomal irinotecan + 5-FU (NAPOLI-1). Targeted therapy: olaparib for germline BRCA-mutated metastatic disease after platinum (POLO trial); pembrolizumab for MSI-H (<1%). NALIRIFOX (liposomal irinotecan + 5-FU/LV) approved first-line based on NAPOLI-3 trial. Supportive care critical: biliary stenting for jaundice, celiac plexus block for pain, pancreatic enzyme replacement, nutritional support, diabetes management.
